primary_catalyst.description: Intel Q1 FY26 earnings (released after close April 23) affirmed hyperscaler CPU demand: $13.6B revenue (vs $12.7B YoY), strongest top-line growth since 2021. DCAI segment +22% YoY to $5.1B. CEO cited three drivers: (1) accelerated Xeon 6 ramp at hyperscalers preparing 2027 capacity, (2) stronger-than-expected commercial PC refresh (Win10 EOL), (3) incremental growth. Revenue beat midpoint of guidance by $1.4B. Validates AI data center buildout as real, not vaporware. Intel +24% (best day since 1987). NVDA closed at $208.27 (+4.32%), first record close since October, market cap >$5T. Semiconductors rose for 18th straight day.

secondary_catalysts.description: CNBC: Nvidia stock closes at record, pushing market cap past $5 trillion â€” 4.3% jump on Intel print
Hyperscaler capex projected ~$650B across MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META/ORCL for 2026 per INTC DCAI context

force_attributions.rationale: Intel DCAI +22% YoY to $5.1B is the cleanest independent Tier 4 validation of hyperscaler AI capex being real and accelerating â€” Xeon 6 hyperscaler ramp for 2027 capacity IS the NVDA-adjacent CPU portion of AI rack spend. This is the dominant A1 force on the day.
AI data center buildout validation also indirectly validates power/grid infrastructure demand â€” CPU ramp implies rack-level buildout is executing
Breakout-day momentum + semis rising 18 straight days drove CTA/trend chase; record-close FOMO amplified the move
Intel's strength is marginally a competitive story (CPU TCO gaining vs pure-GPU rack design) but on this day the AI-validation signal dominated the competitive-threat reading

f1_attribution.rationale: Intel Q1 FY26 is the textbook Tier 4 F1 event: adjacent AI supplier (CPU side of rack) independently corroborating that hyperscaler capex is not only intact but accelerating. This is independent of NVDA itself and directly confirms the A1 demand-side thesis.

notes: CRITICAL â€” TODAY. Single-catalyst day: Intel Q1 FY26 earnings released after close Apr 23 drove gap-up Apr 24 open. F1 Tier 4 multiplier (1.5x) applied to A1 attribution. First NVDA record close since October; market cap >$5T.
